title: Cestrum maleolens
taxonomy: Cestrum maleolens is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Solanaceae, commonly known as the night-blooming jasmine. This classification places it within the order Solanales. The plant is native to Mexico and is often grown for its fragrant, white flowers that bloom in the evening. The family Solanaceae includes a wide range of plants, including tomatoes, potatoes, and eggplants. Cestrum maleolens is a unique member of this diverse family due to its distinctive blooming habits.

Its extracts have been studied for potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which could be beneficial in treating certain health conditions. intro: Cestrum maleolens, also known as the night-blooming cestrum, is a flowering plant species in the nightshade family (Solanaceae) native to South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can help to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and pain. The bioactive constituents of Cestrum maleolens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its pharmacological activities. In herbal medicine, Cestrum maleolens is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is used to treat a range of conditions, including anxiety disorders, insomnia, and digestive issues.
